conjecture_title: Informal conjecture on the generality of the probabilistic minor strategy
paper_title: Clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph
summary: The conjecture is an informal programmatic statement that the randomized star-contraction strategy introduced in arXiv:2002.11100 may find clique minors under other structural restrictions beyond K_s-free and K_{s,t}-free graphs. No subsequent paper has been found that explicitly identifies this strategy as the key tool in a new structural setting, though the broader paradigm of probabilistic methods for clique minors remains active. The statement is methodological rather than a precise mathematical claim, so it cannot be formally proved or disproved.

notes: This entry is an informal methodological remark rather than a precise mathematical conjecture; it cannot be formally resolved as proved or disproved. The paper was published in Random Structures and Algorithms 60 (2022), pp. 327–338. A thorough web search across Semantic Scholar, arXiv, and Google Scholar found no follow-up paper that explicitly credits the star-contraction strategy of Bucić–Fox–Sudakov as the main tool for a new structural restriction. Status is open with high confidence given the recency and absence of any indexed follow-up.
